sanskrit: तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलं यज्ञतपःक्रियाः। दानक्रियाश्च विविधाः क्रियन्ते मोक्षकाङ्क्षिभिः॥
transliteration: tad ity anabhisandhāya phalaṃ yajña-tapaḥ-kriyāḥ | dāna-kriyāś ca vividhāḥ kriyante mokṣa-kāṅkṣibhiḥ ||
literal_meaning: tat—that; iti—thus; anabhisandhāya—without aiming at; phalam—fruit; yajña—sacrifice; tapaḥ—austerity; kriyāḥ—actions; dāna—charity; kriyāḥ—actions; ca—and; vividhāḥ—various; kriyante—are performed; mokṣa—liberation; kāṅkṣibhiḥ—by those who desire
contextual_meaning: Actions of sacrifice, austerity, and charity are done without attachment to results by those who truly seek inner freedom.
psychological_state: A disciplined mind that redirects reward-seeking toward ultimate freedom rather than transient gains.
trigger_scenario: Choosing to volunteer or donate anonymously, feeling fulfilled without praise or tax receipts.
anchor_text: Give, serve, and strive freely, wanting only freedom, not rewards.
